Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- defuddle: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page.
- json-canvas: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.
- obsidian-bases: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.
- obsidian-cli: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.
- obsidian-markdown: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.
- obsidian-vault-organizer: \---
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

input.content:
Function Get-LapsPassword {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Retrieves the Microsoft Entra LAPS password for a specified computer via Microsoft Graph.

    .DESCRIPTION
        Connects to Microsoft Graph using provided credentials (client secret or interactive)
        and retrieves the LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) password for the
        specified device name. Requires the DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All and Device.Read.All
        Graph permissions.

    .PARAMETER ComputerName
        The display name of the device as registered in Entra ID / Azure AD.

    .PARAMETER TenantId
        The Azure AD / Entra ID tenant ID (GUID or domain, e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com).

    .PARAMETER ClientId
        The Application (client) ID of the app registration used for authentication.
        If omitted, interactive (delegated) authentication is used.

    .PARAMETER ClientSecret
        The client secret for the app registration. Required when using app-only authentication.
        If omitted, interactive (delegated) authentication is used.

    .EXAMPLE
        # Interactive / delegated login
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -TenantId "contoso.onmicrosoft.com"

    .EXAMPLE
        # App-only (service principal) authentication
        $secret = Read-Host "Client Secret" -AsSecureString
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" `
                         -TenantId "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx" `
                         -ClientId "yyyyyyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyyyyyyyyyy" `
                         -ClientSecret $secret

    .NOTES
        Author:  Chuck Littlefield
        Date:    2026-04-16

        Prerequisites:
            Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser -Repository PSGallery -Force

        Required Graph permissions (delegated or application):
            Device.Read.All
            DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, HelpMessage = "Display name of the device in Entra ID")]
        [ValidateNotNullOrEmpty()]
        [string]$ComputerName,

        [Parameter(Mandatory, HelpMessage = "Entra ID tenant ID or domain")]
        [ValidateNotNullOrEmpty()]
        [string]$TenantId,

        [Parameter(HelpMessage = "App registration client ID (omit for interactive login)")]
        [string]$ClientId,

        [Parameter(HelpMessage = "App registration client secret (omit for interactive login)")]
        [SecureString]$ClientSecret
    )

    # Verify Microsoft.Graph module is available
    if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name Microsoft.Graph)) {
        throw "Microsoft.Graph module is not installed. Run: Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser -Repository PSGallery -Force"
    }

    try {
        # ── Connect to Microsoft Graph ──────────────────────────────────────────
        if ($ClientId -and $ClientSecret) {
            Write-Verbose "Connecting to Microsoft Graph with app-only credentials..."

            # Convert SecureString to plain text for the credential object
            $bstr   = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::SecureStringToBSTR($ClientSecret)
            $plain  = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::PtrToStringBSTR($bstr)
            [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::ZeroFreeBSTR($bstr)

            $clientSecretCredential = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential(
                $ClientId,
                ($plain | ConvertTo-SecureString -AsPlainText -Force)
            )

            Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $TenantId `
                            -ClientSecretCredential $clientSecretCredential `
                            -NoWelcome `
                            -ErrorAction Stop
        }
        else {
            Write-Verbose "Connecting to Microsoft Graph interactively..."
            Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $TenantId `
                            -Scopes "Device.Read.All", "DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All" `
                            -NoWelcome `
                            -ErrorAction Stop
        }

        Write-Verbose "Connected to Microsoft Graph."

        # ── Find the device in Entra ID ────────────────────────────────────────
        Write-Verbose "Searching for device: $ComputerName"
        $device = Get-MgDevice -Filter "displayName eq '$ComputerName'" -ErrorAction Stop

        if (-not $device) {
            Write-Warning "No device found in Entra ID with the name '$ComputerName'."
            return
        }

        if ($device.Count -gt 1) {
            Write-Warning "$($device.Count) devices matched '$ComputerName'. Using the first result."
            $device = $device[0]
        }

        $deviceId = $device.Id
        Write-Verbose "Found device '$ComputerName' — Object ID: $deviceId"

        # ── Retrieve the LAPS credential ───────────────────────────────────────
        $lapsCredential = Get-MgDeviceLocalCredential -DeviceId $deviceId `
                                                       -Property "credentials" `
                                                       -ErrorAction Stop

        if (-not $lapsCredential -or -not $lapsCredential.Credentials) {
            Write-Warning "No LAPS credentials found for '$ComputerName'. Ensure Windows LAPS is configured and backed up to Entra ID."
            return
        }

        # Build output object — there may be multiple credentials (e.g. rotation history)
        $results = foreach ($cred in $lapsCredential.Credentials) {
            [PSCustomObject]@{
                ComputerName      = $ComputerName
                DeviceId          = $deviceId
                AccountName       = $cred.AccountName
                Password          = $cred.PasswordBase64 | ForEach-Object {
                                        [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString(
                                            [System.Convert]::FromBase64String($_)
                                        )
                                    }
                PasswordUpdatedOn = $cred.BackupDateTime
            }
        }

        return $results
    }
    catch {
        Write-Error "Error retrieving LAPS password: $_"
    }
    finally {
        Disconnect-MgGraph -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
        Write-Verbose "Disconnected from Microsoft Graph."
    }
}

Function Get-LapsPassword {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Retrieves the Microsoft Entra LAPS password for a specified computer via Microsoft Graph.

    .DESCRIPTION
        Connects to Microsoft Graph using provided credentials (client secret or interactive)
        and retrieves the LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) password for the
        specified device name. Requires the DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All and Device.Read.All
        Graph permissions.

    .PARAMETER ComputerName
        The display name of the device as registered in Entra ID / Azure AD.

    .PARAMETER TenantId
        The Azure AD / Entra ID tenant ID (GUID or domain, e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com).

    .PARAMETER ClientId
        The Application (client) ID of the app registration used for authentication.
        If omitted, interactive (delegated) authentication is used.

    .PARAMETER ClientSecret
        The client secret for the app registration. Required when using app-only authentication.
        If omitted, interactive (delegated) authentication is used.

    .EXAMPLE
        # Interactive / delegated login
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -TenantId "contoso.onmicrosoft.com"

    .EXAMPLE
        # App-only (service principal) authentication
        $secret = Read-Host "Client Secret" -AsSecureString
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" `
                         -TenantId "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx" `
                         -ClientId "yyyyyyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyyyyyyyyyy" `
                         -ClientSecret $secret

    .NOTES
        Author:  Chuck Littlefield
        Date:    2026-04-16

        Prerequisites:
            Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser -Repository PSGallery -Force

        Required Graph permissions (delegated or application):
            Device.Read.All
            DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, HelpMessage = "Display name of the device in Entra ID")]
        [ValidateNotNullOrEmpty()]
        [string]$ComputerName,

        [Parameter(Mandatory, HelpMessage = "Entra ID tenant ID or domain")]
        [ValidateNotNullOrEmpty()]
        [string]$TenantId,

        [Parameter(HelpMessage = "App registration client ID (omit for interactive login)")]
        [string]$ClientId,

        [Parameter(HelpMessage = "App registration client secret (omit for interactive login)")]
        [SecureString]$ClientSecret
    )

    # Verify Microsoft.Graph module is available
    if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name Microsoft.Graph)) {
        throw "Microsoft.Graph module is not installed. Run: Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser -Repository PSGallery -Force"
    }

    try {
        # ── Connect to Microsoft Graph ──────────────────────────────────────────
        if ($ClientId -and $ClientSecret) {
            Write-Verbose "Connecting to Microsoft Graph with app-only credentials..."

            # Convert SecureString to plain text for the credential object
            $bstr   = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::SecureStringToBSTR($ClientSecret)
            $plain  = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::PtrToStringBSTR($bstr)
            [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::ZeroFreeBSTR($bstr)

            $clientSecretCredential = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential(
                $ClientId,
                ($plain | ConvertTo-SecureString -AsPlainText -Force)
            )

            Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $TenantId `
                            -ClientSecretCredential $clientSecretCredential `
                            -NoWelcome `
                            -ErrorAction Stop
        }
        else {
            Write-Verbose "Connecting to Microsoft Graph interactively..."
            Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $TenantId `
                            -Scopes "Device.Read.All", "DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All" `
                            -NoWelcome `
                            -ErrorAction Stop
        }

        Write-Verbose "Connected to Microsoft Graph."

        # ── Find the device in Entra ID ────────────────────────────────────────
        Write-Verbose "Searching for device: $ComputerName"
        $device = Get-MgDevice -Filter "displayName eq '$ComputerName'" -ErrorAction Stop

        if (-not $device) {
            Write-Warning "No device found in Entra ID with the name '$ComputerName'."
            return
        }

        if ($device.Count -gt 1) {
            Write-Warning "$($device.Count) devices matched '$ComputerName'. Using the first result."
            $device = $device[0]
        }

        $deviceId = $device.Id
        Write-Verbose "Found device '$ComputerName' — Object ID: $deviceId"

        # ── Retrieve the LAPS credential ───────────────────────────────────────
        $lapsCredential = Get-MgDeviceLocalCredential -DeviceId $deviceId `
                                                       -Property "credentials" `
                                                       -ErrorAction Stop

        if (-not $lapsCredential -or -not $lapsCredential.Credentials) {
            Write-Warning "No LAPS credentials found for '$ComputerName'. Ensure Windows LAPS is configured and backed up to Entra ID."
            return
        }

        # Build output object — there may be multiple credentials (e.g. rotation history)
        $results = foreach ($cred in $lapsCredential.Credentials) {
            [PSCustomObject]@{
                ComputerName      = $ComputerName
                DeviceId          = $deviceId
                AccountName       = $cred.AccountName
                Password          = $cred.PasswordBase64 | ForEach-Object {
                                        [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString(
                                            [System.Convert]::FromBase64String($_)
                                        )
                                    }
                PasswordUpdatedOn = $cred.BackupDateTime
            }
        }

        return $results
    }
    catch {
        Write-Error "Error retrieving LAPS password: $_"
    }
    finally {
        Disconnect-MgGraph -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
        Write-Verbose "Disconnected from Microsoft Graph."
    }
}

content.text:
[Get-LapsPassword.ps1](Powershell/FCTG/Get-LapsPassword.ps1) is written. Here's a summary of what it does:

**Authentication modes**
- **Interactive (delegated):** omit `-ClientId` / `-ClientSecret` — prompts a browser login and requests the required scopes automatically.
- **App-only (service principal):** supply `-ClientId` and `-ClientSecret` (as a `SecureString`) — useful for automation/scheduled tasks.

**What it does**
1. Validates the `Microsoft.Graph` module is installed.
2. Connects with the appropriate auth method.
3. Looks up the device in Entra ID by display name (`Get-MgDevice`).
4. Calls `Get-MgDeviceLocalCredential` to pull the LAPS credential backup.
5. Base64-decodes the password and returns a `PSCustomObject` with `ComputerName`, `DeviceId`, `AccountName`, `Password`, and `PasswordUpdatedOn`.
6. Disconnects from Graph in the `finally` block regardless of outcome.

**Quick usage**
```powershell
# Dot-source and run interactively
. .\Get-LapsPassword.ps1
Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -TenantId "contoso.onmicrosoft.com"

# App-only
$secret = Read-Host "Client Secret" -AsSecureString
Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -TenantId "<tenant-id>" -ClientId "<client-id>" -ClientSecret $secret
```

**Required Graph permissions** (delegated or application): `Device.Read.All` + `DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All`

lastPrompt: create a powershell script the connects to mggraph and retrieves LAPS password of provided computer name using provided credentials
